attributes.name: firstName
attributes.usage: Used in personalized greetings, mail merges, search, and informal address. Combined with last name to form full name in most CRMs. Required-or-not varies: CiviCRM requires at least one of first/last/ email/display_name on Individual contacts. Salesforce, HubSpot, EspoCRM typically allow either first or last name to be empty.

attributes.name: lastName
attributes.usage: Anchors formal address ("Mr. Smith"), alphabetical sorting, and duplicate-detection rules. In Salesforce, last name is technically the only required name field on Contact (first name optional). Some systems' duplicate-detection rules use the combination of last name plus email or last name plus phone.

attributes.description: Primary email address of the individual.
attributes.name: emailAddress
attributes.usage: Universal communication anchor and most common duplicate-detection key. Drives email automation, marketing campaign delivery, password reset flows, and identity verification. CiviCRM and Bloomerang model email as a separate first-class entity (one Contact / Constituent can have many email records with different types: Home, Work, Other). Salesforce, HubSpot, Attio, EspoCRM model email as one or more flat attributes on Contact.

attributes.description: Primary phone number of the individual.
attributes.name: phoneNumber
attributes.usage: Direct line to the individual. Many CRMs support multiple phone attributes (Mobile, Home, Office, Fax, Other) with type discriminators. EspoCRM ships phoneNumber with a typeList of Mobile/Home/Office/Fax/ Other. CiviCRM and Bloomerang model phones as separate entities (one Contact, many Phone records). HubSpot has separate properties for `phone` (general) and `mobilephone`.

attributes.description: Mobile (cell) phone number of the individual.
attributes.name: mobilePhone
attributes.usage: Distinct from main phone for SMS messaging, two-factor authentication, and direct-mobile outreach. Increasingly important as primary contact number for individuals; some deployments collapse phone and mobilePhone into one. In EspoCRM, Mobile is one of the type values on the unified phoneNumber attribute rather than a separate field.

attributes.description: Job title or role of the individual at their associated organization.
attributes.name: title
attributes.usage: Surfaces seniority and decision-making authority for sales targeting. Used in personalized email signatures, mail-merge greetings, and ICP scoring. Typically populated from LinkedIn or self-reported during form fills. Distinct from a Role or Persona attribute that classifies what kind of stakeholder the contact is to your business.

attributes.description: Department or business unit the contact works in.
attributes.name: department
attributes.usage: Subdivides large enterprise Accounts for complex sales motions where multiple departments are buying separately. Less commonly used in mid-market or nonprofit contexts. In NPSP and CiviCRM, more often replaced by Affiliation records that track which department or group the contact belongs to.

attributes.description: The Account / Company / Household / Organization the contact belongs to.
attributes.name: account
attributes.notes: In CiviCRM and Bloomerang, the Account-Contact relationship is modeled via Relationship records (employer, member of household, etc.) rather than a direct foreign key on Contact.
attributes.usage: Anchors the Contact to its parent Account, enabling rolled-up reporting, contextual views ("all contacts at Acme"), and cascading permissions. Cardinality varies: Salesforce's standard model is many-to-one (one Account per Contact), with AccountContactRelation as the optional many-to-many extension. HubSpot supports many-to-many via primary- and-secondary company associations. NPSP requires every Contact to have an Account assignment (will auto-create a Household Account if none specified). Attio uses a relationship attribute "Company" on the Person object.

attributes.description: Composite mailing address for the individual.
attributes.name: mailingAddress
attributes.usage: Used for direct-mail campaigns, physical correspondence, and shipping. CiviCRM and Bloomerang model address as a separate entity allowing multiple addresses per contact (Home, Work, Other, Seasonal). Salesforce uses MailingAddress as a composite (MailingStreet, MailingCity, etc.). HubSpot uses flat individual properties.

attributes.description: Date of birth of the individual.
attributes.name: birthDate
attributes.usage: Powers birthday-acknowledgment workflows, age-based eligibility checks (program enrollment, alcohol-purchasing, senior pricing), and demographic segmentation. Sensitive PII; often gated behind permission sets. In nonprofit deployments commonly used for board-member tenure tracking and volunteer-eligibility verification.

attributes.name: gender
attributes.usage: Used in personalized salutations (Mr/Ms/Mx), demographic reporting, and segmentation for membership organizations. Sensitive PII; many deployments now treat as optional with a "Prefer not to say" or free- text option. CiviCRM and EspoCRM ship with built-in gender enum options; Salesforce and HubSpot do not have a standard Gender field and require it as a custom property.

attributes.description: Flag indicating the contact has opted out of all communication.
attributes.name: doNotContact
attributes.usage: Critical compliance flag for GDPR, CAN-SPAM, TCPA, and CASL. When set, should suppress this contact from all outbound campaigns, automation flows, and bulk imports. Some systems break this into channel-specific flags (DoNotEmail, DoNotCall, DoNotSMS) rather than a single catch-all. Bloomerang uses CommunicationRestrictions as a multi-value field. Required to be respected in essentially every modern CRM deployment for legal-compliance reasons.

attributes.description: Flag indicating the contact has unsubscribed from email communication.
attributes.name: emailOptOut
attributes.usage: Channel-specific opt-out flag. Set automatically when a contact clicks an unsubscribe link in a marketing email. Distinct from DoNotContact because a contact may still accept transactional or relationship communication (donation receipts, account alerts) while opting out of marketing.

attributes.description: The user who owns or is responsible for the contact.
attributes.name: assignedUser
attributes.usage: Same role as Account.assignedUser. Drives sharing rules, "my contacts" filtering, and assignment automation. In nonprofit contexts often represents the assigned development officer or relationship manager.

attributes.description: System-generated timestamp of when the record was created.
attributes.name: createdAt
attributes.usage: Audit and analytics field. Drives "new contacts this month" reports and acquisition-cohort analysis. Always read-only and system-managed.

attributes.description: System-generated timestamp of when the record was last updated.
attributes.name: lastModifiedAt
attributes.usage: Audit field. Used in change-tracking workflows and integration sync filters (only pull contacts modified since last sync).

business_context: Contact records are where CRMs hold information about people. In B2B sales motions, Contacts are typically associated with an Account (the company they work for) and act as the points of communication for opportunities, cases, and marketing campaigns. In nonprofit deployments, Contacts are often the primary record (donors, volunteers, beneficiaries, members) and may exist independently of any Account, or may be grouped into Households rather than Companies. The decision of whether a person belongs primarily to a Contact or to a Lead (in systems that distinguish them) drives pipeline and conversion metrics. The decision of whether a Contact must belong to an Account, may belong to many Accounts, or stands alone shapes the entire data model. When interviewing clients, surface "Contact" by asking which categories of people they need to track: customers, donors, volunteers, mentees, applicants, alumni, board members, staff, vendors' staff, and so on. Each category may map to a Contact subclass (donor, volunteer, etc.) or to a Tag/Type discriminator. Personal-data protection rules (GDPR, CCPA, HIPAA) typically apply at the Contact level, so consent, communication preferences, and do-not-contact flags are fundamental.
name: Contact
purpose: An individual person the CRM tracks: customer contact, prospect, donor, volunteer, mentor, board member, staff member, or any other natural person whose information needs to be stored. Together with Account, one of two universal anchor entities in CRM data models.

relationships.description: A contact typically belongs to one Account / Company / Household. Some systems support many-to-many via secondary associations.
A contact has many activities (calls, meetings, tasks, emails) logged.
A contact has many notes attached to it.
Contact-to-contact relationships (spouse, parent, colleague, mentor/ mentee, member of household). Modeled via Relationship records or Affiliation records depending on system.

sources.title: Attio: Manage standard objects (People system attributes)
Salesforce NPSP: Organize Your Data Effectively Using NPSP Features
Bloomerang REST API documentation: Constituent endpoint

systems.name: Person
systems.notes: Built-in standard object, enabled by default. Attio uses "People" rather than "Contacts." Auto-creates from email syncing and meeting records. Many attributes are enriched (job title, social media, first/last interaction, communication intelligence).

systems.name: Constituent (Individual Type)
systems.notes: Same Constituent entity as Account. Type=Individual selects this subtype. Individual-only attributes include FirstName, LastName, Prefix, Suffix, Birthdate, Gender. Households are not first-class — modeled via Relationships between individual constituents.

systems.name: Individual (Contact subtype)
systems.notes: Same Contact entity as Account; contact_type=Individual selects this subtype. Individual-only fields (first_name, last_name, gender, birth_date, prefix, suffix, nickname, job_title) only apply when contact_type=Individual. Required fields differ by subtype: Individual requires at least one of first_name/last_name/email/display_name.

systems.name: Contact
systems.notes: Standard CRM-module entity. Distinct from Lead (which converts to Contact + Account + Opportunity). Includes built-in addresses, multiple phone types, and stream/follow capability.

systems.name: Contact
systems.notes: Standard object. HubSpot uses Contact as a unified concept — there is no separate Lead entity; lead-vs-customer status is a `lifecyclestage` property on Contact. Contacts can associate with multiple companies and deals.

systems.name: Contact
systems.notes: Standard object. Salesforce distinguishes Lead (unconverted prospect) from Contact (qualified or current relationship). Lead converts into a new Account + Contact + Opportunity triple. Person Account is an optional alternate model that combines Account + Contact for B2C use.

systems.name: Contact
systems.notes: Standard Salesforce Contact, with NPSP additions: every Contact is automatically associated with an Account (Household, 1x1, or Organization Record Type). NPSP adds rollup fields for lifetime giving totals, last donation date, recurring giving status. Uses Affiliation records to track a Contact's connections to Organization Accounts (employer, board membership, etc.) separately from their primary Household Account.
